title: Redesign Your Engineering Hire Process: The Loop You’ve Never Heard About
excerpt: Redesign your engineering interview process by focusing on real-world problem-solving and collaboration skills, not just coding tests.
body_html:
The modern hiring landscape in tech is fraught with inefficiencies. While most companies focus on resume screening, initial interviews, coding challenges, and final assessments, one crucial phase often gets overlooked: the onboarding loop.

THE ONBOARDING LOOP: WHAT IT IS AND WHY IT MATTERS

The onboarding loop is a series of informal interactions between candidates and current employees. It’s where the company culture truly shines through. Despite its importance, it's seldom included in formal interview processes.

  • Identifying cultural fit
  • Evaluating teamwork skills
  • Maintaining candidate interest throughout the process
  • Providing a realistic preview of the job role and company environment

A well-designed onboarding loop can significantly improve hiring outcomes, making candidates more confident about their decision to join your team. Here’s how you can integrate it into your hiring pipeline.

ESTABLISHING REAL-WORLD SCENARIOS

The key to a successful onboarding loop is incorporating real-world problem-solving exercises and collaborative tasks. This approach helps candidates understand the day-to-day challenges they will face, giving them a clearer picture of their potential role.

  • Case studies: Present hypothetical scenarios that require technical and soft skills to solve
  • Pair programming sessions: Allow candidates to work with current team members on small tasks
  • Group projects: Facilitate collaborative problem-solving exercises where the candidate can interact with the team

These activities not only provide valuable insights but also build rapport and trust between potential hires and existing employees.

BUILDING CULTURAL AWARENESS THROUGH MENTORING AND BUDGETING

Another critical aspect of the onboarding loop is cultural awareness. Ensuring that candidates are aware of your company’s values, mission, and overall culture is essential for long-term success.

  • Mentorship programs: Pair candidates with experienced team members who can guide them through their first days and weeks
  • Cultural workshops: Organize sessions to explain the history, current state, and future goals of your company
  • Team-building activities: Plan off-site events or online sessions that foster a sense of community and collaboration

A structured approach helps candidates feel more at ease and prepared for their new roles. It also gives them an opportunity to ask questions and express any concerns they might have.

FEEDBACK AND IMPROVEMENT CYCLES

The onboarding loop isn’t a one-time event; it’s an ongoing process that benefits both candidates and the organization. Regular feedback loops are crucial for continuous improvement.

  • Candidate surveys: Send out anonymous questionnaires to gather candid feedback about their experience and suggestions for improvements
  • Team member evaluations: Have existing employees provide constructive feedback on how well the candidate is adapting and contributing
  • Iterative process refinement: Use collected data to refine your interview and onboarding processes, ensuring they remain effective over time

A structured way of collecting and acting upon feedback ensures that both the hiring team and candidates are better prepared for future roles.

CONCLUSION

The onboarding loop is a critical phase often missed in the traditional interview process. By integrating real-world scenarios, building cultural awareness through mentorship, and establishing robust feedback cycles, you can significantly enhance your hiring outcomes.
